claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for, and if so, how many children are there?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to claim Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

To assess eligibility for each child, I need to ask a few questions. Let me start with the basics:

How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3 years old, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 10 - all under 16, which is good.

Next question: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And do all three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie - live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Charlie live with me, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Alex and Charlie live with you, but Blake doesn't.

For Blake, who is 8 years old and doesn't live with you - are you paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after Blake? This could include things like food, clothes, or pocket money.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially to Blake's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Just to confirm a few more details: Are any of the children currently in hospital, care, or any other living arrangement that I should know about? And are Alex and Charlie living with you full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually Blake has been in hospital for a few months now. But I do regularly spend money on things for him - like magazines, snacks, and things he needs while he's there. Alex and Charlie are living with me full-time, yes.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that - this is important information. So Blake has been in hospital for a few months, but you are regularly spending money on him.

Can you tell me more specifically how long Blake has been in hospital? Has it been more than 12 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's been more than 12 weeks. It's been about 18 weeks now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Blake has been in hospital for 18 weeks (which is more than 12 weeks), but you mentioned you're regularly spending money on him - that's an important factor.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Is Blake in a UK hospital, and approximately how much are you spending on him regularly (roughly per week or month)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's a UK hospital. I spend money on him regularly - buying things like magazines, his favourite snacks, clothes, and other bits he needs while he's there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me make sure I have everything correct:

- Alex (3 years old) lives with you full-time in the UK
- Blake (8 years old) has been in a UK hospital for 18 weeks, but you regularly spend money on him for magazines, snacks, clothes, and other things he needs
- Charlie (10 years old) lives with you full-time in the UK

Is there anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's the breakdown for each child:

**Alex (3 years old):** ✓ Eligible
- Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you're responsible for them. You meet all the standard eligibility criteria.

**Blake (8 years old):** ✓ Eligible  
- Even though Blake has been in hospital for 18 weeks (which is more than the usual 12-week limit), there's an important exception: you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child in a UK hospital if you regularly spend money on them. Since you're regularly buying magazines, snacks, clothes, and other things Blake needs while in hospital, you qualify for this exception.

**Charlie (10 years old):** ✓ Eligible
- Charlie is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you're responsible for them. You meet all the standard eligibility criteria.

Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of your children, you can proceed with your claim for all three. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website or by contacting HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex. The claimant meets the basic eligibility criteria as they live with Alex and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16) and has been in a UK hospital for 18 weeks, which exceeds the 12-week limit. However, there is an exception to the rule: the claimant can continue to be entitled to Child Benefit for children in a UK hospital if they regularly spend money on them. The claimant confirmed they regularly spend money on Blake for magazines, snacks, clothes, and other necessities while in hospital. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, the claimant qualifies for the exception and is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Charlie. The claimant meets the basic eligibility criteria as they live with Charlie and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10). Alex and Charlie live with the claimant full-time and meet the standard eligibility criteria. Blake has been in a UK hospital for 18 weeks (exceeding the 12-week threshold), but the claimant regularly spends money on Blake while in hospital, which qualifies for an exception to the rule. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
